description: A UTF-8 encoded, growable string. This is the most common string type in Rust. Python equivalent: str (but owned and mutable).

methods.name: from
methods.description: Creates a String from a string slice.

methods.name: push_str
methods.description: Appends a string slice to this String.
methods.example:
let mut s = String::from("hello");
s.push_str(" world");

methods.name: is_empty
methods.description: Returns true if the string has zero length.

methods.name: contains
methods.description: Returns true if the pattern matches a sub-slice.

methods.name: replace
methods.description: Replaces all matches of a pattern with another string.

methods.name: trim
methods.description: Returns a string slice with leading and trailing whitespace removed.
